title: agrostis alba subsp. vulgaris
intro: Agrostis alba subsp. vulgaris, also known as green bent or rough bent, is a subspecies of grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its coarse, rhizomatous stems and green or yellowish-green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Agrostis alba subsp. vulgaris include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is also used in traditional folk medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
